content_of_post_to_answer: How can you train yourself to stop reacting immediately in stressful situations?
answer: One shift that helped me was realizing that reacting fast and responding well are different skills. Stress creates the illusion that every feeling is urgent, but most aren't. If you can delay your first response by even a few seconds, you often avoid fixing problems your own reaction created. I've explored this pattern in more depth on my Quora profile if you're curious. The pause isn't hesitation; it's where judgment catches up with emotion.
